channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Freezing hikers stuck in waist-deep snow rescued during Mount Washington whiteout
item.description: Two experienced hikers were rescued after an hourslong ordeal in which they became stranded in waist-deep snow and whiteout conditions on New Hampshire's challenging Mount Washington.
item.dc:creator: Kathy Mccormack And Michael Casey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Protesters in cities across the US rally against Trump's policies, Project 2025 and Elon Musk
item.description: Demonstrators gathered in cities across the U.S. on Wednesday to protest the Trump administration's early actions, decrying everything from the president's immigration crackdown to his rollback of transgender rights and a proposal to forcibly transfer Palestinians from the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A Texas man is executed for the killing of a pastor during a robbery at a church
item.description: HUNTSVILLE, Texas (AP) — A Texas man convicted of beating and suffocating a Dallas area pastor in his church during a robbery was put to death Wednesday evening, the second execution in the U.S. this year and the first of four scheduled in Texas over the next three months.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CIA shares new employee names through unclassified email
item.description: The CIA used unclassified email to share a list of employees hired within the last two years to the Office of Personnel Management (OPM).
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump OPM buyout offers leave federal workers with risky decisions
item.description: The Trump administration and federal employee unions are in a head-to-head lobbying battle as workers face a Feb. 6 deadline to decide whether they want to accept a government buyout.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Musk says DOGE will make 'rapid safety upgrades' to air traffic control system
item.description: "With the support of President @realDonaldTrump, the @DOGE team will aim to make rapid safety upgrades to the air traffic control system," Musk wrote Wednesday on the social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is targeting antisemitism in schools. Experts fear other civil rights will be ignored
item.description: The federal office that enforces civil rights at school across the nation has been ordered to prioritize complaints of antisemitism above all else as it molds to President Donald Trump’s agenda, raising fears that other rights violations will go unpunished.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley And Bianca Vázquez Toness, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Police seek motive after a suspect was arrested for a fatal shooting at an Ohio cosmetics warehouse
item.description: NEW ALBANY, Ohio (AP) — A man accused of shooting to death a coworker and wounding five others at an Ohio warehouse has spoken with police as they probe for a motive, authorities said Wednesday, hours after he was arrested in an apartment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Rare invasive bug intercepted at Detroit airport
item.description: The seed bugs, which grow up to a half-inch in length, are most typically found in southern Europe and parts of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmie 'Jay' Lee remains found in Memphis, police confirm
item.description: On Wednesday, the Mississippi Crime Lab confirmed through DNA analysis that the remains belong to "Jay" Lee.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, NewsNation

item.title: Japan Airlines plane clips Delta flight at Sea-Tac airport
item.description: A plane at Seattle-Tacoma International Airport hit another aircraft that was taxiing on the runway Wednesday morning, the Federal Aviation Administration has confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man jailed over police AI program, then freed 17 months after victim raised doubts
item.description: Chris Gatlin spent 17 months in jail for a crime that an artificial intelligence program said he committed, only to be freed after the prosecutor learned there was no real evidence.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, Fox 5

item.title: Rare invasive bug intercepted at Detroit airport
item.description: Agriculture specialists with U.S. Customs and Border Protection confirm that rare invasive seed bugs were intercepted on undeclared cargo late last year at Detroit Metro Airport.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, Fox 5

item.title: Armed teen arrested at Arkansas airport after allegedly demanding plane
item.description: After the employees got to safety, the suspect "breached through a door onto the airfield," an airport official said.
item.dc:creator: Hunter Trombetta, NewsNation

item.title: Delphi murders: State's filing includes photos of Libby German's iPhone 6s
item.description: In November, a jury convicted Allen of murder in the February 2017 deaths of Abby Williams and Libby German near the Monon High Bridge.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: WATCH: Crews save unresponsive man in spinning boat on Florida lake
item.description: A man found unresponsive inside a boat that was driving in circles was saved by Florida authorities on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, KRON4

item.title: Suspect arrested after one dead, 5 injured in New Albany mass shooting
item.description: For the latest updates in New Albany warehouse shooting, follow this link. NEW ALBANY, Ohio (WCMH) -- A man is in custody after one person was shot dead and five others were injured after a shooting Tuesday night at a New Albany warehouse. According to authorities, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Mills, ABC 4

item.title: Pam Bondi sworn in as attorney general in Oval Office
item.description: “I know I'm supposed to say, ‘She's going to be totally impartial with respect to Democrats.’ And I think she will be as impartial as a person can be,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Decorated pilot Harry Stewart, Jr., one of the last surviving Tuskegee Airmen, dies at 100
item.description: Retired Lt. Col. Harry Stewart Jr, a decorated World War II pilot who broke racial barriers as a Tuskegee Airmen and earned honors for his combat heroism, has died. He was 100.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In bid for Davos-like gathering, Semafor plans to bring at least 200 CEOs to Washington conference
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The media company Semafor is planning a Washington economic conference for global CEOs this April that it hopes will rival the annual World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, for influence.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 accused in Aurora kidnapping, torture appear in court
item.description: Three men accused of kidnapping and torturing a couple at an Aurora apartment complex were in court Monday.
item.dc:creator: Vicente Arenas, NewsNation

item.title: Suspect arrested after shooting at Ohio cosmetics facility leaves 1 dead, 5 injured
item.description: Officials announced that Foster had been arrested by U.S. Marshalls after a manhunt that stretched through the night.
item.dc:creator: Brett Mills, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wind and water are slowly taking this luxury house overlooking Cape Cod Bay
item.description: The waters of Cape Cod Bay are coming for the big brown house perched on a sandy bluff high above the beach.
item.dc:creator: Andre Muggiati, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: Both U.S. allies and adversaries alike rejected President Donald Trump’s suggestion that the U.S. “take over” the war-torn Gaza Strip and turn it into the “Riviera of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: 2 arrested in connection to string of high-end burglaries, including one at Joe Burrow's home
item.description: The men have been accused of selling the stolen goods pilfered from "wealthier neighborhoods" and, according to sources, the home of Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: VIDEO: 2 men facing charges following courtroom brawl in Albuquerque
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (KRQE) – A routine court hearing devolved into chaos when two people in the gallery rushed at the defendant and ended up starting a brawl. Now, those two men are facing charges. It happened at a district court hearing Friday morning for murder suspect Alexander Ortiz.
item.dc:creator: Chandler Farnsworth, KRON4

item.title: Biden signs with powerful Hollywood agency
item.description: Is former President Joe Biden Hollywood's next leading man? The 46th president has signed with behemoth talent firm Creative Artists Agency to handle his professional life after leaving office, the agency announced.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: Major League Baseball fires ump amid gambling probe
item.description: Umpire Pat Hoberg was fired after it was revealed he shared betting accounts with a friend who bet on baseball.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Gerber recalls all batches of Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks nationwide
item.description: Gerber Products Company has announced a nationwide recall and discontinuation of all batches of its Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks due to a potential choking hazard for infants and young children.
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, KTLA 5

item.title: Duffy tells DOT to prioritize areas with high birth rates
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy in a memo told the Department of Transportation (DOT) to prioritize communities that have higher birth and marriage rates.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Tariff threats take aim at fentanyl trafficking. Here's how the drug reaches the US
item.description: President Donald Trump's plan to impose tariffs on goods from Mexico, Canada and China is partly aimed at combating the illicit flow of fentanyl into the U.S., where the opioid is blamed for some 70,000 overdose deaths annually. Mexico agreed Monday to send 10,000 troops to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Transportation secretary says 'expansive' impact zone was left by Philadelphia medical plane crash
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The impact area where a medical transportation plane crashed in Philadelphia was notably “expansive,” U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy said Monday after viewing the site with investigators and city officials. Duffy said he was struck by “how expansive it was.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump kept his tariffs pledge. Is he ready for the fallout? Are Americans? What to know
item.description: The Budget Lab at Yale University estimates Trump’s tariffs would cost the average American household $1,000 to $1,200 in annual purchasing power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Army identifies 3rd soldier killed in DC crash
item.description: The U.S. Army on Saturday identified the third soldier who died last week during a training mission that ended in a midair crash between an Army Black Hawk helicopter and an American Airlines regional jet near Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: EU warns Trump it would ‘respond firmly’ to tariffs
item.description: “The EU would respond firmly to any trading partner that unfairly or arbitrarily imposes tariffs on EU goods,” a spokesperson said. “There is a lot at stake.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Families visit DC crash site days after the deadliest US air disaster in a generation
item.description: Dozens of people walked along the banks of the Potomac River near Reagan National Airport, close to where an American Airlines jet and an Army Black Hawk helicopter collided Wednesday, killing all 67 aboard.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A medical plane carrying a child patient and 5 others crashes in Philadelphia, setting homes ablaze
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — A medical transport jet with a child patient, her mother and four others aboard crashed into a Philadelphia neighborhood shortly after takeoff Friday evening, exploding in a fireball that engulfed several homes.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MATT ROURKE, Associated Press, WGN 9
